# revolut_webhook (Resource)

A Revolut Merchant webhook. url and events are mutable in place (PATCH). The signing_secret is server-generated and sensitive; changing rotate_trigger rotates it. Max 10 webhooks per merchant.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "revolut_webhook" "orders" {
  url    = "https://example.com/webhooks/revolut"
  events = ["ORDER_COMPLETED", "ORDER_AUTHORISED"]

  # Change this keeper to rotate the signing secret. expiration_period sets the
  # grace window during which the previous secret stays valid.
  rotate_trigger    = "v1"
  expiration_period = "PT5H30M"
}

output "webhook_signing_secret" {
  value     = revolut_webhook.orders.signing_secret
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `events` (Set of String) Event types to subscribe to (e.g. ORDER_COMPLETED). Mutable in place.
- `url` (String) HTTPS endpoint that receives webhook events. Mutable in place.

### Optional

- `expiration_period` (String) ISO-8601 grace window for the previous secret during rotation (e.g. PT5H30M).
- `rotate_trigger` (String) Arbitrary keeper value. Changing it triggers a signing-secret rotation.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Server-assigned webhook UUID.
- `signing_secret` (String, Sensitive) Server-generated signing secret (wsk_...). Sensitive; never an input.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Webhooks support import by their UUID. Read recovers url, events, and the
# signing_secret.
terraform import revolut_webhook.orders 11111111-2222-3333-4444-555555555555
```
